//: This file controls how your Verilog code is converted into a physical silicon layout.
It provides parameters for synthesis, placement, routing, and timing closure.
!!! DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE unless you know what you are doing !!!
PL_TARGET_DENSITY_PCT - How tightly packed the logic gates are in the die area.
Values around 60-80 are common. If placement fails (congestion), decrease this.
CLOCK_PERIOD - The target clock speed in nanoseconds.
20ns == 50MHz. Increase this value if the design fails to meet timing (setup violations).
Hold slack margin - Safety buffers to prevent 'hold time' violations.
Enable/Disable linting and PDK model inclusion.
Identify which port should be treated as the system clock.
Synthesis Strategy - Controls how the synthesizer optimizes the logic.
AREA 3 focuses on minimizing the number of gates.
Margin multipliers define the space between the logic and the edge of the tile.
I/O Pin and Power Grid parameters.
Clock Tree Synthesis (CTS) - Required for stable clock distribution.
